2603.17524.title: KineVLA: Towards Kinematics-Aware Vision-Language-Action Models with Bi-Level Action Decomposition
2603.17524.paper_summary: In this paper, we introduce a novel kinematics-rich vision-language-action (VLA) task, in which language commands densely encode diverse kinematic attributes (such as direction, trajectory, orientation, and relative displacement) from initiation through completion, at key moments, unlike existing action instructions that capture kinematics only coarsely or partially, thereby supporting fine-grained and personalized manipulation. In this setting, where task goals remain invariant while execution trajectories must adapt to instruction-level kinematic specifications. To address this challenge, we propose KineVLA, a vision-language-action framework that explicitly decouples goal-level invariance from kinematics-level variability through a bi-level action representation and bi-level reasoning tokens to serve as explicit, supervised intermediate variables that align language and action. To support this task, we construct the kinematics-aware VLA datasets spanning both simulation and real-world robotic platforms, featuring instruction-level kinematic variations and bi-level annotations. Extensive experiments on LIBERO and a Realman-75 robot demonstrate that KineVLA consistently outperforms strong VLA baselines on kinematics-sensitive benchmarks, achieving more precise, controllable, and generalizable manipulation behaviors.
2603.17524.paper_summary_zh: 在本文中，我們介紹了一個新穎的運動學豐富的視覺-語言-行動（VLA）任務，其中語言命令密集編碼了多樣的運動學屬性（例如方向、軌跡、方位和相對位移），從開始到完成，在關鍵時刻，與現有的僅粗略或部分捕捉運動學的行動指令不同，從而支持細緻和個性化的操作。在這種情境中，任務目標保持不變，而執行軌跡必須適應指令級的運動學規範。為了解決這一挑戰，我們提出了KineVLA，一個視覺-語言-行動框架，通過雙層行動表示和雙層推理標記明確地將目標級不變性與運動學級變異性解耦，以作為明確的、有監督的中介變量，對齊語言和行動。為了支持這項任務，我們構建了運動學感知的VLA數據集，涵蓋了模擬和現實世界的機器人平台，具有指令級的運動學變異和雙層標註。在LIBERO和Realman-75機器人上的廣泛實驗表明，KineVLA在運動學敏感的基準上始終優於強大的VLA基線，實現了更精確、可控和可泛化的操作行為。
2603.17524.author: Gaoge Han et.al.
2603.17524.authors: Gaoge Han, Zhengqing Gao, Ziwen Li, Jiaxin Huang, Shaoli Huang, Fakhri Karray, Mingming Gong, Tongliang Liu
